message: Node started: Using the probability P calculated by helper_1, calculate the expected number of intersection points for N = 25 chords. Note that the expected number of intersections is the sum of the probabilities of intersection for all possible pairs of chords. Since the chords are chosen under the same quadrant-constraint distribution, use the linearity of expectation. Then, apply the formula for the number of regions R created by N chords in a circle, which is R = 1 + N + I, where I is the number of intersection points, assuming no three chords are concurrent. State any assumptions regarding the probability of triple intersections.
